_methodology: Wh per 1,000 tokens, chip energy only (PUE applied separately by the calculator). Derivation anchor: joules-per-output-token estimates for GPU serving of open-weight models (EcoLogits methodology; 1 J/token = 0.278 Wh/1k tokens). Input (prefill) tokens processed in parallel, costed at ~10% of output (decode) per token. VERIFY these v0 values against EcoLogits published figures during build hours 0-2. Note stated in methodology view: Groq serves on custom LPU hardware; factors model a representative GPU deployment.
_labels: every number derived from these factors is ESTIMATED

groq:llama-3.1-8b-instant.source: derived: ~0.43 J/output-token for 8B-class dense on GPU serving (EcoLogits-consistent)

groq:llama-3.3-70b-versatile.source: derived: ~3.6 J/output-token for 70B-class dense on GPU serving (EcoLogits-consistent)

groq:moonshotai/kimi-k2-instruct.source: derived: ~5 J/output-token; MoE activates ~32B params but full weights occupy memory across many devices

gemini:gemini-2.5-flash.source: assumption: fast mid-tier closed model, treated as ~20-70B-class serving cost; params undisclosed by Google
gemini:gemini-2.5-flash.assumptions: STATED GUESS — judge-model overhead, counted against Ember's totals (D4)

openai:gpt-4o.source: assumption: frontier-class closed model, treated as >= kimi-k2 energy; params undisclosed by OpenAI
openai:gpt-4o.assumptions: STATED GUESS — surface params_known=false in methodology view

anthropic:claude-opus-4-8.source: assumption: frontier-class closed model; params undisclosed by Anthropic
anthropic:claude-opus-4-8.assumptions: STATED GUESS — surface params_known=false in methodology view
